evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/offroad.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site presents Discover, Investigate, and Remediate agents for identity risk across human users, service accounts, OAuth applications, machine identities, and AI agents.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: OhAuth exposes a public catalog for OAuth apps across Google Workspace, GitHub, Slack, Zoom, Atlassian, Microsoft 365, Salesforce, and other ecosystems.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: Offroad's OAuth report lists over-permissioned apps, dead publisher domains, buyable domains, and AI apps with broad write access as risk categories.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether autonomous identity remediation can move fast while still satisfying approval, audit, and ownership requirements.
TODO: State the most important weekly evidence gap or product risk without guessing.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/freshflow.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site breaks the product into Forecast, Inventory, Optimization, and Freshflow App modules for fresh-food replenishment decisions.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: Freshflow publishes customer-facing outcome claims including 120+ demand signals, 93% order-proposal acceptance, up to 30% less waste, and up to 4% more revenue.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether each retailer's POS, spoilage, promotion, and scan-error data is clean enough for reliable store-level automation.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/willow.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site shows discovery for AI agents, tools, and MCP servers, plus shadow AI detection and usage analytics.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: Willow describes build/runtime guards, permissioned tool access, audit trails, IdP connection, Slack/Jira/GitHub connectors, and API-to-MCP conversion.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether teams adopt a control layer early enough, before unmanaged agent usage creates a visible incident.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/scotch.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site presents POS, payments, inventory, ordering, receiving, analytics, invoice reading, and offline selling as core surfaces.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The liquor-store product page describes case-break tracking, distributor invoice automation, shrinkage reports, age verification, and AI ordering tools.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether vertical depth remains durable if broad POS incumbents clone the highest-value liquor workflows.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/airspeed.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site describes agents for call prep, follow-ups, CRM hygiene, coaching plans, and integrations with Salesforce, HubSpot, Slack, Gmail, Outlook, Zoom, and Teams.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The Series A story says Airspeed is approaching 200 customers and frames the product around context layers, sub-agent orchestration, and Customer 360.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether enterprise buyers will allow autonomous agents to act inside revenue operations instead of keeping them in draft-only mode.

evidence.label: VentureDex product record
evidence.source: content/startups/truefan-ai.json summary, editor_note, funding, and research fields

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 1
evidence.source: The official site positions TrueFan AI as an enterprise platform for personalized AI videos in 175+ languages, with a single recording used across campaigns.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 2
evidence.source: The Studio login exposes a production app surface with Google, Microsoft, and email sign-in paths for creating AI avatar and personalized video workflows.

evidence.label: Structured product evidence 3
evidence.source: The BFSI page describes lead generation, cross-sell, referrals, customer retention, and training use cases plus SOC 2 and ISO 27001 security claims.

risks: Research risk candidate: The open question is whether enterprise buyers will consistently approve AI-avatar output for regulated or high-trust brand communications.
